title: Pan con Tomate (Pa amb Tomàquet)

overview.description: A classic Catalan breakfast of toasted bread rubbed with garlic and ripe tomato, finished with olive oil and flaky sea salt.

method: Toast the bread until crisp but still slightly chewy in the centre.
Lightly rub one side of each slice with the garlic.
Rub the cut side of each tomato firmly over the bread until most of the flesh has been transferred.
Drizzle generously with extra virgin olive oil.
Finish with flaky sea salt and a little black pepper if desired.

learning.did-you-know: Pa amb Tomàquet has been enjoyed in Catalonia for well over a century and is often served from breakfast through to dinner.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How high-quality ingredients and simple techniques can produce outstanding flavour.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An incredibly simple breakfast that celebrates good bread, ripe tomatoes and excellent olive oil while using ingredients almost always found in the kitchen during summer.
chapman-tips: Use the ripest tomatoes you can find—they're the star of the dish.
Don't be tempted to spread the tomato with a spoon. Rubbing the tomato into the bread gives the traditional texture.
Finish with your best extra virgin olive oil.
Eat immediately while the toast is still crisp.
If your tomatoes aren't at their best, leave them on the kitchen counter for a day or two rather than refrigerating them. Cold tomatoes lose much of their flavour.
